MINI MINUTES FOR APR-12-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LR PARTNERS L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE PARTIES' CROSS-MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DISPUTE THE MEANING OF THE "LANG RANCH PROPERTY CATV SYSTEM AGREEMENT" ("UTLX AGREEMENT") THAT THEIR PREDECESSORS IN INTEREST (UTLX AND SCCTV) ENTERED INTO IN 1969. (BURT DECL., EX. 1.) PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT LR PARTNERS, L.L.C. ("LR") IS THE SUCCESSOR TO UTLX AND DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT SPECTRUM PACIFIC WEST, L.L.C. ("SPECTRUM") IS THE SUCCESSOR TO SCCTV. THE COURT GRANTS LR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. (SEE WORD MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) ORDER ON THE CROSS-MOTIONS IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

MINUTES FOR APR-12-2021 9:30 AM

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS CHARTER COMMUNICATIONS, INC., SPECTRUM PACIFIC WEST, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: SPECTRUM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS MOOT AS TO ISSUES TWO THROUGH SEVEN AND TEN. THE RULINGS ON LR'S CROSS-MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RESOLVE THESE ISSUES AGAINST SPECTRUM. THE MOTION IS ALSO MOOT AS TO ISSUES EIGHT AND NINE (DEFENSES) GIVEN THAT THE COURT HAS DETERMINED THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SUBSTANTIVELY LACKS MERIT. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON ISSUE ONE AND DISMISSES CHARTER FROM THIS CASE. ORDER ON THE CROSS-MOTIONS IS SIGNED. (SEE MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-26-2021 Continued To Feb-16-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Marie Jonas, Esq. of Folger Levin, 415.625.1050, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant LR Partners LLC;
Atty. Abraham Shanedling, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, 202.747.2189, for Defendant Charter Communications, Inc., Defendant/Cross-Complainant Spectrum Pacific West, LLC.

PLAINTIFF LR PARTNERS L.L.C.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

The parties' cross-motions for summary adjudication dispute the meaning of the "Lang Ranch Property CATV System Agreement" ("UTLX Agreement") that their predecessors in interest (UTLX and SCCTV) entered into in 1969. (Burt Decl., Ex. 1.) Plaintiff/cross-defendant LR Partners, L.L.C. ("LR") is the successor to UTLX and defendant/cross-complainant Spectrum Pacific West, L.L.C. ("Spectrum") is the successor to SCCTV.

The Court grants LR's motion for summary adjudication in its entirety.

The Court grants summary adjudication on issue 1 as Spectrum owes a duty to make the 16 2/3% payments in conformance with the UTLX Agreement and Spectrum's attempts to avoid that duty founder. (See Linden Partners v. Wilshire Linden Assocs. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 508, 519 [court may summarily adjudicate issue of contractual duty].)

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order on the cross-motions is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Charter Communications, Inc.S Spectrum Pacific West, Llcs Cross-Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-12-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Marie Jonas, Esq. of Folger Levin, 415.625.1050, for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant LR Partners LLC;
Atty. Abraham Shanedling, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, 202.747.2189, for Defendant Charter Communications, Inc., Defendant/Cross-Complainant Spectrum Pacific West, LLC.

DEFENDANTS CHARTER COMMUNICATIONS, INC., SPECTRUM PACIFIC WEST, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Spectrum's motion for summary adjudication is denied as moot as to issues two through seven and ten. The rulings on LR's cross-motion for summary adjudication resolve these issues against Spectrum. The motion is also moot as to issues eight and nine (defenses) given that the Court has determined that the cross-complaint substantively lacks merit. To the extent the defenses are still live, the Court would grant summary adjudication against LR as it does not address those issues in its points and authorities and Burt's admissions demonstrate that the defenses cannot be established. The Court grants summary adjudication on issue one and dismisses Charter from this case. The undisputed evidence shows that Spectrum is the successor on the UTLX Agreement. (LR's Opposing Separate Statement, UMF 14, 15 ["Spectrum, not Charter, is SCCTV's successor-in-interest."].) LR cites no authority to support its proposition that Charter should be liable for the debt because Charter was confused regarding the payment obligations when it purchased Time Warner and Charter's counsel indicated that payment would be forthcoming. A parent corporation like Charter is normally not responsible for its subsidiary's debts. (See Northern Natural Gas Co. v. Superior Court (1976) 64 Cal.App.3d 983, 991 ["A parent corporation is not liable on the contract or for the tortious acts of its subsidiary simply because it is a wholly owned subsidiary."].)

Order on the cross-motions is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Mar-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHARTER COMMUNICATIONS, INC. and cross-plaintiff SPECTRUM PACIFIC WEST, LLC's application for order allowing Paul Werner to appear as counsel PRO HAC VICE.

The application of defendant Charter Communications, Inc. and Cross-Complainant Spectrum Pacific West, LLC to allow Paul Werner to appear as counsel pro hac vice is granted. No opposition filed and the application conforms to CRC 9.40.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
